name: construct-control-panel
description: Operator console for a Construct agent VM — lifecycle, updates, usage, projects, and microphone passthrough, from inside VS Code.

construct.scriptsDir.markdownDescription: Folder that holds the Construct PowerShell lifecycle scripts (`Auto-Install.ps1`, `projects/`, `.construct-settings.json`). Leave empty to auto-detect the newest install under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\The-Construct`.

construct.debug.markdownDescription: Diagnostics: keep launched host consoles OPEN (`-NoExit`) so errors stay readable instead of flashing closed, and log verbosely. Every launch is also recorded (with the exact command, resolved paths, args, and result) to the **Construct** Output channel — run **The Construct: Show Logs** to view it.

enumDescriptions: Not chosen yet — the design picker opens the next time a Construct panel is shown.
Classic Matrix — the original green operator console.
Terminal, Refined — the Matrix identity with a disciplined phosphor palette; glow only on live elements.
VS Code Native — looks like a built-in panel and follows your editor theme (light or dark).
construct.uiTheme.markdownDescription: Visual design of the Construct panels. Every design has identical functionality — only the styling changes. Pick with preview images via **The Construct: Choose UI Design**.

construct.micDevice.markdownDescription: Windows microphone passthrough: the exact DirectShow capture-device name to record from. Leave empty to auto-detect the first audio device. List devices with `ffmpeg -list_devices true -f dshow -i dummy` and copy a name from the DirectShow audio section (e.g. `Microphone (Realtek(R) Audio)`).
